methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 53.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Gravitics is a Seed-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Colin Doughan that has raised $20M plus $60M via STRATFI financing. The broader Space & Aerospace sector comprises 23 private peers and 6 public comparables including RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, and SATL; however, narrow peer clustering at equivalent stage and valuation tier remains sparse.

Given data constraints, a single triangulation method proves applicable. The Stage-Progression approach uses the $20M raised proxy as a valuation anchor and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, implying a post-money valuation of $50M. Peer-Median analysis was not feasible due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables in the narrow peer set (fewer than three datapoints met filtering criteria). Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be executed because target revenue remains undisclosed, which is standard for private companies; the sector median P/S of 53.2x across five public tickers is therefore not actionable here.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $42M to $57M, with a median of $50M. This band reflects reasonable sensitivity around the stage-progression multiple rather than independent method convergence. Key uncertainties include the true revenue or production scale of Gravitics relative to public peers, the materiality of the $60M STRATFI facility to post-money valuation treatment, and whether comparable stage-multiples in aerospace differ materially from broader tech cohorts.
